mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer spending and business investment, which jointly amplify equity cash flow expectations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO rises month-over-month compared to periods of flat or declining INDPRO (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy faster than anticipated, increasing real interest rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL accelerates by more than 0.5% over a rolling three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the total addressable market for corporate revenues, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and stagnant federal funds rates indicates a favorable macroeconomic environment where labor demand outpaces monetary tightening, jointly boosting equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS level fails to explain variance in 20-day returns beyond individual series effects, or forward returns are negative when PAYEMS rises and FEDFUNDS remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 120 signals an overheated supply side that typically precedes capacity constraints and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 120, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for UNRATE.
